steps.title: Convert the percent loss to a decay factor
steps.content: Losing $22\%$ of the mass each period leaves $100\%-22\%=78\%$, so the per-period multiplier is $0.78$ — not $0.22$.

steps.title: Set up the exponent
steps.content: One decay period equals $11.11$ minutes, so after $t$ minutes the number of completed periods is $\dfrac{t}{11.11}$. The model is $$M=100\,(0.78)^{t/11.11}.$$ Check: at $t=11.11$, $M=100(0.78)^{1}=78$ grams, a $22\%$ drop, as required.

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• A) $M=100(0.22)^{t+11.11}$ — uses the wrong base ($0.22$ instead of $0.78$) and a nonsensical exponent. Eliminated.
  • B) $M=100(0.22)^{t/11.11}$ — correct exponent form but wrong base; would lose $78\%$ per period. Eliminated.
  • C) $M=100(0.78)^{t+11.11}$ — correct base but the exponent $t+11.11$ has the wrong units and gives the wrong value at $t=0$. Eliminated.
  • D) $M=100(0.78)^{t/11.11}$ — correct base and correct exponent.
That is the correct answer.
